summary: Visual capture of the live Flux flow-field on the default Aurora palette, verifying the artwork and control panel render.

content.objective: Verify that the Flux flow-field renders a full-viewport particle artwork on the Aurora palette, with the control panel showing the Aurora palette name.
actions: Wait a few seconds for the flow-field canvas to paint
validations: A full-viewport particle flow-field artwork is visible on the canvas
The control panel displays the palette name Aurora

summary: Visual capture of the Flux flow-field after switching to the Ember palette.

content.objective: Verify that selecting the Ember palette repaints the flow-field artwork and the control panel shows the Ember palette name.
actions: Select Ember from the Palette selector dropdown
Wait for the canvas to repaint in the new palette
validations: The control panel displays the palette name Ember
A full-viewport particle flow-field artwork is visible on the canvas

summary: Palette selector functionality on the live animation, verifying that choosing Ember updates the displayed palette name.

content.objective: Verify that selecting the Ember option in the palette selector updates the panel to display the Ember palette name.
actions: Select Ember from the Palette selector dropdown
validations: The palette name readout in the control panel displays Ember

summary: Pause and resume control on the live animation, verifying the Pause button toggles its label to Resume when the animation is paused.

content.objective: Verify that clicking the Pause button pauses the animation and toggles the button label to Resume.
validations: The button that previously read Pause now reads Resume

summary: Particle-count input on the live animation, verifying that typing a new value into the Particles control and pressing Enter updates the displayed particle count.

content.objective: Verify that entering a new particle count and pressing Enter updates the displayed particle-count value in the control panel.
actions: Type 3000 into the Particles number input
validations: The particle-count value shown next to the Particles label displays 3000
